title: Retro Gaming
description: Classic games from the golden era

questions.question: What year was Pac-Man first released?
questions.explanation: Pac-Man was released by Namco in 1980 and became an arcade icon.

questions.question: Which game was bundled with the original Game Boy?
questions.explanation: Tetris was bundled with the Game Boy when it launched in 1989.

questions.question: What is the name of Mario's brother?
questions.explanation: Luigi is Mario's younger, taller brother who first appeared in Mario Bros. (1983).

questions.question: Which company created Sonic the Hedgehog?
questions.explanation: Sonic was created by Sega as their mascot to compete with Nintendo's Mario.

questions.question: What year was the original Doom released?
questions.explanation: Doom was released in December 1993 and revolutionized the FPS genre.

questions.question: In the original Street Fighter II, which country is Ryu from?
questions.explanation: Ryu is a Japanese martial artist and one of the most iconic fighting game characters.

questions.question: What was the first home console by Nintendo?
questions.explanation: The NES (Nintendo Entertainment System) launched in 1983 in Japan as the Famicom.

questions.question: Which game featured a princess named Peach who needed rescuing?
questions.explanation: Princess Peach (originally called Toadstool) is rescued in Super Mario Bros.

questions.question: What year was the original Grand Theft Auto released?
questions.explanation: The original GTA was released in 1997 with a top-down perspective.

questions.question: Which game introduced the 'Konami Code' (Up Up Down Down...)?
questions.explanation: The Konami Code first appeared in Gradius (1986), but became famous through Contra.

questions.question: What was Half-Life developer Valve's home city?
questions.explanation: Valve Corporation is headquartered in Bellevue, Washington.

questions.question: Which 1996 game let players capture and train creatures?
questions.explanation: Pokemon Red and Blue launched in Japan in 1996 and became a global phenomenon.

questions.question: What was the best-selling console of the 1990s?
questions.explanation: The original PlayStation sold over 102 million units, dominating the 90s.

questions.question: Which classic game has the goal of clearing lines by fitting falling blocks?
questions.explanation: Tetris was created by Alexey Pajitnov in 1985 and became one of the most iconic games ever.

questions.question: In what year was The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time released?
questions.explanation: Ocarina of Time launched in 1998 on N64 and is considered one of the greatest games ever.
